D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Cochineal — 70,000 Insects per Pound of Carmine

Aztec dye from Dactylopius coccus scale insects yields carminic acid; Spanish colonial monopoly made it the New World's second-largest export after silver

Carminic acid is an anthraquinone — the same chromophore family as madder's alizarin. Cochineal feeds on prickly-pear cacti; harvesters scraped insects off the pads and dried them. 70,000 insects ≈ 1 lb of dye. The Spanish crown enforced a monopoly for 250 years.

Slide pH; carminic acid shifts orange (acidic) → red → violet (alkaline).
